appStack.description: The application stack name, used for naming resources and grouping applications

language.description: Programming language used by the application

oneOf.description: Name of the git branch (used for feature branches)
Label for the branch, used for reference label

forceAutoscaling.description: Force autoscaling to be enabled regardless of environment

name.description: Environment name (dev, preprod, prod, qa, uat, or feature*)

oneOf.description: Override the Kubernetes namespace (defaults to environment.name if not set)
Environment name for dependencies
Whether to override the default domain
Domain to use when override is enabled
Enable Vault CA certificate download during pod startup
Disable OpenTelemetry automatic instrumentation
Default tolerations for all applications

isEnvironmentDeploy.description: Whether this is an environment-level deployment. When true, suppresses the offload ApplicationSet generator and enables staticHostname to be added to VirtualService hosts in feature environments.

oneOf.description: Whether this is a frontend application
Whether to force offloading

Static hostname for the application. In non-feature environments, replaces the default appstack-appname hostname. In feature environments, only added to the VirtualService hosts when isEnvironmentDeploy is true.
Custom labels to add to the application resources
Custom annotations to add to the application resources

ttlSecondsAfterFinished.description: Time to keep test job resources after completion (seconds)

activeDeadlineSeconds.description: Maximum time for the test job to run (seconds)

backoffLimit.description: Number of retries for the test job

apikey.description: API key for the test engine, can be a direct value or a vault reference

webhook_url.description: URL of the test engine webhook, can be a direct value or a vault reference

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for the test job pod

containerImage.description: Container image to use for running tests

secretId.description: Reference to secrets needed for tests, can be a direct value or a vault reference

filters.description: Test filters to select which tests to run. Accepts a single string or an array of strings.

serviceAddress.description: Optional service address for the test to target

additionalEnvVars.description: Additional environment variables to pass to the test container

tolerations.description: Tolerations for test pods scheduled by the test engine

nodeAffinity.description: Node affinity rules for test pods scheduled by the test engine

useDefaultNodeAffinity.description: Whether to use default node affinity for test pods. Accepts boolean or string.

spreadAcrossNodes.description: Whether to spread test pods across nodes. Accepts boolean or string.

legacyMode.description: Enable legacy mode for test execution. Accepts boolean or string.

podResources.description: Resource requests and limits for test pods scheduled by the test engine

oneOf.description: Whether the application is migrating to Argo Rollouts
Application version string
Application version as object
Environment variable from source
Image pull policy
Name of the pull secret

command.description: Command to run in the container

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command

subPath.description: Subpath within the volume to mount

configMapName.description: Name of the configMap (if kind is configMap)

items.description: Items to include from the configMap

internalEnabled.description: Enable internal ServiceEntry, WasmPlugin, and VirtualService internal host for feature-routing via WASM fallback. When false, these resources are not rendered and feature-header requests fall through to the default route.

offloadVSEnabled.description: Enable the offload VirtualService for feature environments. When false, the offload VS is not rendered for this application.

offloadOperatorEnabled.description: Enable offload-operator managed routing. When true, the standard VirtualService and offload VS are suppressed, and OffloadBase/OffloadRoute CRDs are rendered instead. Defaults to true when metaEnvironment is dev, false otherwise. Set explicitly to override.

offloadCreationPolicy.description: CreationPolicy for the OffloadBase CRD. Controls how the operator handles the target VirtualService.

offloadVirtualServiceAnnotations.description: Annotations to apply to the operator-managed VirtualService.

offloadVirtualServiceLabels.description: Labels to apply to the operator-managed VirtualService.

routes.description: Custom routes for the VirtualService. Each key is used as the route name. By default, routes destination is the owning application's service. Use the optional 'destination' field to override.

destination.description: Optional destination host override. If not specified, defaults to the owning application's service FQDN.

port.description: Optional destination port override. If not specified, defaults to the application's ports.http value (or 8080).

allowedEndpoints.description: List of allowed HTTP endpoints for the VirtualService. Can be strings with wildcards or objects with kind and match fields.

oneOf.description: Endpoint path as a string. If it contains *, it will be treated as a prefix match, otherwise as an exact match.
Endpoint definition with explicit kind (prefix, exact, or regex) and match pattern.

kind.description: Type of URI matching: prefix for path prefixes, exact for exact matches, regex for regular expressions.

match.description: The URI pattern to match against incoming requests.

krakend.description: KrakenDAutoConfig CR configuration. When enabled=true the chart renders a gateway.krakend.io/v1alpha1 KrakenDAutoConfig for the application. Requires a non-feature environment, networking.istio.enabled=true, networking.istio.internalEnabled=true, and neither istioDisabled nor service.istioDisabled may be true. See the helm.krakend.enabled helper for the exact gating.

enabled.description: Render a KrakenDAutoConfig CR for this application.

name.description: Override the KrakenDAutoConfig metadata.name. Defaults to <fullName>-autoconfig.

autoConfigNamespace.description: Namespace the KrakenDAutoConfig CR is rendered into. Defaults to gatewayRef.namespace → global.krakendGatewayNamespace → application namespace.

name.description: KrakenDGateway metadata.name to attach generated endpoints to.

namespace.description: Gateway namespace. Falls back to global.krakendGatewayNamespace or the application namespace.

interval.description: Poll interval (Go duration, e.g. 5m). Required when trigger=Periodic.

openapi.description: OpenAPI spec source. By default the URL is auto-generated from the application's `.internal` address using `path`. To bypass auto-generation, set `url` (or `configMapRef`) directly; the supplied value is passed through to the CR.

url.description: Explicit OpenAPI spec URL. When set, overrides the auto-generated http://<app>.<metaEnv>.internal<path> URL. Must point to an internal address (.internal or .svc.cluster.local) and must not contain the external domain.

path.description: Path portion appended to http://<app>.<metaEnv>.internal. Ignored when `url` is set. Defaults to /swagger/v1/swagger.json.

configMapRef.description: Load the OpenAPI spec from a ConfigMap key instead of fetching a URL.

auth.description: Optional authentication configuration for the spec fetch (BearerTokenSecret or BasicAuthSecret).

hostMapping.description: Direct passthrough of KrakenDAutoConfigSpec.urlTransform.hostMapping. Equivalent to extraHostMapping; provided so users can paste rendered CR values verbatim. Each `to:` value must point to an internal address and must not contain the external domain.

extraHostMapping.description: Alias for hostMapping retained for backward compatibility. Entries are appended to any `hostMapping` entries. Each `to:` value must not contain the external domain.

defaults.description: Default values for generated endpoints and backends. Mirrors KrakenDAutoConfigSpec.Defaults.

postStart.description: Command to run on container post-start

preStop.description: Command to run on container pre-stop

keda.description: KEDA autoscaler configuration for Azure Service Bus scaling

enabled.description: Enable KEDA ScaledObject for this application

topicName.description: Azure Service Bus topic name (required when type is 'topic')

subscriptionName.description: Azure Service Bus subscription name (required when type is 'topic')

queueName.description: Azure Service Bus queue name (required when type is 'queue')

clusterAuthRef.description: Name of an existing ClusterTriggerAuthentication resource (managed outside this chart). Defaults to servicebus-connectionstring-<env> based on environment (dev/feature -> dev, preprod -> preprod, prod -> prod).

messageCount.description: Target message count per replica to trigger scaling

activationMessageCount.description: Message count threshold to activate the scaler (scale from 0)

pollingInterval.description: How often KEDA checks the trigger source (seconds)

cooldownPeriod.description: Period to wait after last trigger before scaling down (seconds)

idleReplicaCount.description: Number of replicas when idle (set to 0 to enable scale-to-zero)

advanced.description: Advanced KEDA scaling configuration (e.g., scaling policies)

env.description: Environment variables as key-value pairs or references

timeZone.description: Time zone for the cron schedule (e.g., 'UTC', 'America/New_York')

concurrencyPolicy.description: How to handle concurrent job executions

successfulJobsHistoryLimit.description: Number of successful jobs to keep in history

failedJobsHistoryLimit.description: Number of failed jobs to keep in history

startingDeadlineSeconds.description: Deadline in seconds for starting the job if it misses scheduled time

labels.description: Custom labels to add to the cronjob resources

annotations.description: Custom annotations to add to the cronjob resources

activeDeadlineSeconds.description: Maximum time for the job to run (seconds)

backoffLimit.description: Number of retries before considering job failed

restartPolicy.description: Restart policy for the job pods

imagePullSecret.description: Name of the image pull secret

command.description: Command to run in the container

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command

env.description: Environment variables as key-value pairs or references

configMapName.description: Name of the ConfigMap to use for environment variables

secretName.description: Name of the Secret to use for environment variables

volumes.description: Volumes to mount in the cronjob

mountPath.description: Path where the volume will be mounted

subPath.description: Subpath within the volume to mount

configMapName.description: Name of the ConfigMap (if kind is configMap)

items.description: Items to include from the ConfigMap

oneOf.description: Path to secrets in vault
Null value when no vault path is needed
Null value when no bulk secrets are needed
Path to the secret in vault
Key in the secret
Name for the secret
Filename for the mounted secret
Path to the secret in vault
Property in the secret
Subpath within the mount
Null value when no subPath is needed
Null value when no secrets are needed

defaults.description: Default values shared across all Azure Crossplane resources

location.description: Default Azure region for all resources (e.g., 'East US'). Empty string means per-resource location is required.

managementPolicies.description: Crossplane management policies (default: ['Observe']). 'Delete' and '*' (wildcard) are blocked.

managedBy.description: ID of the resource that manages this resource group

namespace.description: Custom Kubernetes namespace (default: environment.name, or environment.namespaceOverride if set)

location.description: Azure region for new storage accounts

name.description: Name for new resource group (auto-generated if omitted)

providerConfigRef.description: Crossplane ProviderConfig name, inherited by all child resources (default: 'default')

capacity.description: Messaging units for Premium SKU (1, 2, 4, 8, or 16)

resourceGroupName.description: Resource group name (default: inf-{env}, e.g. inf-dev)

resourceGroupNameRef.description: Crossplane reference to a ResourceGroup resource

resourceGroupNameSelector.description: Crossplane selector for a ResourceGroup resource

managementPolicies.description: Crossplane management policies (default: ['Observe']). 'Delete' and '*' (wildcard) are blocked.

namespace.description: Custom Kubernetes namespace (default: environment.name, or environment.namespaceOverride if set)

topics.description: Service Bus topics (at least one required)

namespaceId.description: Azure resource ID of the Service Bus namespace

subscriptions.description: Optional topic subscriptions. Omit this field or provide an empty array to create a topic without subscriptions.
